threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look puzzled when someone asks if you want your toast 'sourdough or rye' at the café. It’s a dead giveaway.
Caught the N137 last night; a guy in a leopard print coat was debating life choices with a potted plant. The driver didn’t bat an eye.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I spotted a guy brewing beer in his living room while chatting with a neighbor about the latest BVG delays. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab stand that doubles as an art gallery, where the chef also hosts poetry nights. It's a true local gem.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local if they don't flinch at the Muni delays but get mad if you talk trash about the burritos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just watched a dog stroll down the Civic Center track while buses honked in frustration. Only in San Francisco do we stop for furry VIPs.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every bar in Gràcia serves tapas, but many locals head straight for the patates braves at the tiny spot near the square. It's a game changer.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their constant hesitation at the metro ticket machines, still too shy to ask for help.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot the newbies by how they still think they can catch the Tube during a strike. It’s like watching someone try to walk on water.
Last night on the 47, a guy was offering to sell mixtapes. Sounded like a remix of every late-night tube station announcement—totally bizarre.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the smell of grilled döner wafted through my window at midnight, I knew I was home. Nothing says Neukölln like a late-night kebab fix.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a currywurst at 3 AM while dodging spontaneous street concerts. The vibe is unique—never a dull moment.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local when they casually mention their go-to taco spot has a secret menu, while newcomers stick to the tourist traps.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today that stopped for 10 minutes while a guy argued with the driver about his dog’s fare. Classic Tenderloin moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we all hang out at the same bars. The locals know the best spots are the little ones tucked away down side streets, like Xènia or La Vermuteria.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their enthusiasm for a 'tapas crawl'—nobody else tries to hit five bars in one night like that.
